title: Johnson & Johnson — Hematology — briefing Q3-2025
html:
What Changed in J&J Hematology — Q3 2025

Quarter: July–September 2025 Earnings reported: J&J October 14 | Sanofi October 2025 | BMS October 2025 | Pfizer October 2025 Generated: 2026-04-27

1. TL;DR

  1. DARZALEX hit $3.67B in Q3 (+21.7%) — the anti-CD38 franchise is growing faster than Keytruda at this stage of its lifecycle, driven by frontline share capture (PERSEUS + MAIA).
  2. TECVAYLI reached $177M (+31.3%) — bispecific finding footing in late lines, but the 2L story depends on MajesTEC-3 readout.
  3. BMS's Abecma flat at ~$150M — stagnation while CARVYKTI grows signals the CAR-T duopoly is becoming a monopoly.
  4. Revlimid declined to $1.3B Q3 ($5.6B annual run rate) — generic lenalidomide is eating the backbone; MM economics are shifting.
  5. Pfizer's Elrexfio at ~$45M — growing off tiny base; no combination data to compete with J&J's bispecific platform.

2. J&J Financial Signals

Product Q3 2025 YoY Growth
DARZALEX $3.67B +21.7%
CARVYKTI ~$400M Strong
TECVAYLI $177M +31.3%
TALVEY $122M +60.8%
Total MM ~$4.4B ~$17.5B annualized
  • DARZALEX growth is frontline-led: PERSEUS (transplant-eligible) + MAIA (transplant-ineligible) driving share gains
  • TALVEY at +60.8% is the fastest-growing product — post-BCMA rescue positioning is working
  • Heme MSL effectiveness study kicked off (June 2025): 240 planned interactions across NDMM, RRMM, CAR-T

What J&J management said on the Q3 2025 call:

  1. "DARZALEX is the foundational asset of our hematology franchise" — management used "foundational" language that positions DARZALEX as the backbone of the entire MM portfolio, not just a single product. Every other hematology asset (TECVAYLI, TALVEY, CARVYKTI) is framed as building on the DARZALEX foundation.
  2. "DARZALEX is in its mid-cycle, not late cycle" — this is a direct response to market concerns about growth deceleration. Management is signaling that frontline penetration (PERSEUS, MAIA) still has significant runway, countering any narrative that DARZALEX is approaching maturity.
  3. Four growth pillars named: DARZALEX, TREMFYA, ERLEADA, SPRAVATO — DARZALEX was listed first among J&J's company-wide growth drivers. The portfolio-level positioning reinforces that hematology remains J&J's largest and most important franchise.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts challenged CARVYKTI manufacturing capacity — the most pointed question in hematology was about CARVYKTI site scaling. J&J's response ("capacity is scaling, 4 manufacturing sites globally") was factual but didn't address the demand-supply gap timeline. Analysts view manufacturing, not clinical data, as CARVYKTI's growth constraint.
  2. No analyst questioned DARZALEX's growth sustainability — at +21.7%, the Street is comfortable with DARZALEX's trajectory. The "mid-cycle" framing went unchallenged, suggesting consensus alignment with J&J's growth narrative.
  3. TALVEY received minimal analyst attention — despite being the fastest-growing product at +60.8%, no analyst asked about TALVEY specifically. This validates the "underappreciated" thesis — the Street is focused on DARZALEX and CARVYKTI while TALVEY builds quietly.

3. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q3 2025

Company Brand Q3 2025 YoY Growth Key Signal
Sanofi Sarclisa ~$240M +15% Growing but scale gap widening vs. DARZALEX
BMS Abecma ~$150M Flat Manufacturing + safety limiting growth
BMS Revlimid ~$1.3B -30% Generic erosion accelerating
Pfizer Elrexfio ~$45M +60% Tiny base; late-line monotherapy only
AbbVie Imbruvica ~$750M -20% CLL declining to Brukinsa; not MM

Sanofi — Sarclisa (~$240M, +15%)

Sanofi Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Sarclisa growing but falling further behind DARZALEX: At $240M vs. DARZALEX's $3.67B, the scale ratio worsened to ~6.5%. Sanofi highlighted IKEMA long-term data (Sarclisa-Kd PFS2 benefit) but acknowledged the "competitive intensity in anti-CD38" without naming DARZALEX directly.

  2. IsKia trial is the bet: Sanofi emphasized IsKia (Sarclisa-KRd in NDMM) as "the study that defines Sarclisa's potential in frontline." If positive, Sarclisa has a claim in the largest MM population. Enrollment complete; readout expected 2026.

  3. Sanofi diversifying beyond MM: Sanofi's oncology narrative is shifting to Dupixent (immunology) and new pipeline assets. Sarclisa is important but not franchise-defining for Sanofi the way DARZALEX is for J&J.

Implication for J&J: Sarclisa's growth is not a threat at current scale. Monitor IsKia for any frontline efficacy signal that could give payers a negotiation tool.

BMS — Abecma ($150M, flat) + Revlimid ($1.3B, -30%)

BMS Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Abecma stagnation is the headline: Flat at ~$150M while CARVYKTI grows rapidly signals the competitive dynamic has shifted. BMS cited "manufacturing optimization" as a focus — code for: they can't make enough Abecma and CARVYKTI is winning the capacity battle.

  2. T-cell malignancy safety signal: BMS acknowledged the FDA class-wide investigation into T-cell malignancies post-CAR-T. While both Abecma and CARVYKTI carry the risk, Abecma's earlier safety profile concerns (CRS severity, neurological toxicity) compound the narrative. Oncologists defaulting to CARVYKTI where both are available.

  3. Revlimid decline reshaping MM foundation: At -30% YoY, Revlimid is in free fall. BMS management acknowledged the "inevitable transition to generics" and reframed Revlimid as "cash flow generation" rather than a growth asset. The downstream impact: as lenalidomide becomes cheap, the COST of maintaining patients on DARA-Rd (DARZALEX + lenalidomide) drops dramatically — a tailwind for J&J.

  4. BMS Reblozyl as a heme bright spot: Reblozyl (~$350M Q3, +70%) in MDS/beta-thalassemia is BMS's growing hematology asset — but completely different patient population from MM.

Implication for J&J: Abecma's stagnation + Revlimid's decline = BMS retreating from the MM market they defined for 20 years. CARVYKTI is winning the CAR-T battle by default. Revlimid genericization helps J&J's DARA-Rd cost story.

Pfizer — Elrexfio (~$45M, +60%)

Pfizer Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Elrexfio growing but off a tiny base: At $45M quarterly, Elrexfio is viable as a late-line monotherapy but cannot compete with J&J's bispecific portfolio (TECVAYLI at $177M + TALVEY at $122M = $299M combined).

  2. Pfizer framing Elrexfio as "pipeline value": Management cited MagnetisMM-7 (Elrexfio + DARZALEX) as the key trial that will define Elrexfio's commercial potential. Until that data reads out, Elrexfio remains a 4L+ monotherapy option.

  3. Pfizer's oncology investment is elsewhere: Padcev (bladder), Ibrance lifecycle, and ADC pipeline receive the bulk of Pfizer's oncology attention. Elrexfio is an important but not franchise-defining asset.

Implication for J&J: No immediate competitive concern. MajesTEC-3 (TEC-DARA) readout expected Q4 will likely further widen the gap if the data is as strong as expected.

Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us

  1. J&J's MM dominance is widening, not narrowing — every competitor is either flat (Abecma), niche (Sarclisa), tiny (Elrexfio), or declining (Revlimid/Imbruvica). J&J's $17.5B annualized franchise has >10x the revenue of all MM competitors combined.

  2. The CAR-T market is consolidating to J&J — CARVYKTI growing while Abecma stagnates. The duopoly is becoming a monopoly. Manufacturing capacity, not competitor efficacy, is CARVYKTI's ceiling.

  3. Revlimid genericization is a systemic shift — cheap lenalidomide reduces MM backbone costs by 60-70%, making DARZALEX-Rd the cost-efficient frontline standard and creating headroom for bispecifics/CAR-T in later lines.

  4. MajesTEC-3 is the next inflection point — expected Q4 2025 readout. If TEC-DARA shows 2L benefit, no competitor has an equivalent dataset. The bispecific + anti-CD38 combination space becomes J&J's monopoly.

4. Clinical/Regulatory

  • DARZALEX: PERSEUS driving transplant-eligible adoption; MAIA established in transplant-ineligible
  • TECVAYLI: MajesTEC-3 (TEC-DARA in 2L) readout expected Q4 2025 — the most important pending data
  • CARVYKTI: Site expansion ongoing; KarMMa-3 data driving preference over Abecma
  • T-cell malignancy investigation: FDA class-wide; both CAR-T products affected
  • IsKia (Sanofi): Sarclisa-KRd in NDMM Phase 3; enrollment complete

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Evidence This Quarter
1. TEC-DARA reshapes 2L UNCHANGED Awaiting MajesTEC-3 readout (Q4)
2. DARZALEX is the annuity STRENGTHENED $3.67B, +21.7%; Sarclisa at ~6.5% of scale
3. External competition declining NEW — STRENGTHENED Abecma flat, Revlimid declining, Elrexfio tiny, BMS retreating
4. Community adoption INSUFFICIENT DATA MSL effectiveness study just kicked off (June)
5. TALVEY underappreciated STRENGTHENED +60.8% fastest-growing product in MM portfolio

6. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q4 2025)

  1. MajesTEC-3 Phase 3 readout — the most important data event in MM this year
  2. ASH 2025 (December) — DARZALEX frontline, bispecific combos, CARVYKTI expansion
  3. CARVYKTI site expansion pace — manufacturing is the growth bottleneck
  4. Heme MSL study first results — targeted Oct 2025 readout
  5. Abecma Q4 — continued stagnation cements CARVYKTI's lead

7. Conversation Starters for J&J

  1. "BMS's Abecma is flat while CARVYKTI grows. BMS's Revlimid is declining 30%. The company that defined MM treatment for 20 years is exiting. You're inheriting their market. The question is: are you building the tracking infrastructure to own this transition, or just measuring individual products in isolation?" — Frames the competitive shift.

  2. "MajesTEC-3 data is the single most important event for your heme franchise this year. If the 2L data is strong, TEC-DARA becomes the first bispecific approved for early relapse. No competitor has equivalent data. We should be designing the post-approval tracking study NOW so we're ready to measure adoption on day one." — Forward-looking BD tied to data timeline.

  3. "TALVEY at +61% growth is your quiet winner. As more patients cycle through BCMA-targeted therapies (TECVAYLI, CARVYKTI, Elrexfio), the post-BCMA pool grows. TALVEY's GPRC5D mechanism is uniquely positioned. Should we be tracking TALVEY sequencing patterns specifically?" — Surfaces the underappreciated asset.

Sources: J&J Q3 2025 (Oct 14), Sanofi Q3 2025 (Oct 2025), BMS Q3 2025 (Oct 2025), Pfizer Q3 2025 (Oct 2025).

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home

Revlimid decline reshaping MM foundation: At -30% YoY, Revlimid is in free fall. BMS management acknowledged the "inevitable transition to generics" and reframed Revlimid as "cash flow generation" rather than a growth asset. The downstream impact: as lenalidomide becomes cheap, the COST of maintaining patients on DARA-Rd (DARZALEX + lenalidomide) drops dramatically — a tailwind for J&J.


BMS Reblozyl as a heme bright spot: Reblozyl (~$350M Q3, +70%) in MDS/beta-thalassemia is BMS's growing hematology asset — but completely different patient population from MM.

Pfizer Q3 2025 call (October 2025):


Elrexfio growing but off a tiny base: At $45M quarterly, Elrexfio is viable as a late-line monotherapy but cannot compete with J&J's bispecific portfolio (TECVAYLI at $177M + TALVEY at $122M = $299M combined).

Implication for J&J: No immediate competitive concern. MajesTEC-3 (TEC-DARA) readout expected Q4 will likely further widen the gap if the data is as strong as expected.
Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us


J&J's MM dominance is widening, not narrowing — every competitor is either flat (Abecma), niche (Sarclisa), tiny (Elrexfio), or declining (Revlimid/Imbruvica). J&J's $17.5B annualized franchise has >10x the revenue of all MM competitors combined.


The CAR-T market is consolidating to J&J — CARVYKTI growing while Abecma stagnates. The duopoly is becoming a monopoly. Manufacturing capacity, not competitor efficacy, is CARVYKTI's ceiling.


Revlimid genericization is a systemic shift — cheap lenalidomide reduces MM backbone costs by 60-70%, making DARZALEX-Rd the cost-efficient frontline standard and creating headroom for bispecifics/CAR-T in later lines.


MajesTEC-3 is the next inflection point — expected Q4 2025 readout. If TEC-DARA shows 2L benefit, no competitor has an equivalent dataset. The bispecific + anti-CD38 combination space becomes J&J's monopoly.

CARVYKTI: Site expansion ongoing; KarMMa-3 data driving preference over Abecma
T-cell malignancy investigation: FDA class-wide; both CAR-T products affected
IsKia (Sanofi): Sarclisa-KRd in NDMM Phase 3; enrollment complete
